title: The clock that runs slow
summary: Describe an old clock that loses time each day and reflect on how a wrong clock changes the way we feel about time.
topic: An old clock that is always a little slow or fast
prompt: Write 8–10 short sentences about a clock at home that does not show the right time. Say where it is, how much it is wrong, and how your family lives with it. Reflect on what a wrong clock teaches you about time. Use present simple, past simple, and adverbs of frequency.
hintStarters: In our kitchen there is an old clock that…
Every day it loses about… minutes, but…
I think a slow clock teaches me that…

hintVocab.meaningVi: tiếng tích tắc / kêu tích tắc

sampleText: In our kitchen there is an old clock on the wall. It is round and white and it ticks softly. But this clock is always a little slow. Every day it loses about five minutes. So in one week it is very wrong. My grandmother bought this clock many years ago. We know it is slow, so we add ten minutes in our heads. My father often says the kitchen clock lies, but he laughs when he says it. Last month I changed the battery, but the clock was still slow. The problem is inside, not the battery. We never throw it away because we love its soft sound. A new clock is cheap, but this one has a story. I think a slow clock is good in a strange way. It teaches me that time is not a perfect machine. Sometimes I am in a hurry, and the slow clock makes me feel calm. It says, you have more time than you think. I always smile when I look at it.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: an old clock on the wall
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: một chiếc đồng hồ cũ trên tường
nó kêu tích tắc nhẹ nhàng
nó chậm mất khoảng năm phút

sampleAnnotations.phrase: we add ten minutes in our heads
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: chúng tôi cộng thêm mười phút trong đầu
chiếc đồng hồ nhà bếp nói dối
Chúng tôi không bao giờ vứt nó đi

sampleAnnotations.phrase: time is not a perfect machine
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: thời gian không phải là một cỗ máy hoàn hảo

sampleAnnotations.phrase: you have more time than you think
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: bạn có nhiều thời gian hơn bạn nghĩ

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Adverbs of frequency and third-person -s together
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Câu thường có cả trạng từ tần suất và động từ ngôi thứ ba số ít: 'it ticks softly', 'it always a little slow', 'my father often says'. Trạng từ đứng trước động từ chính, và động từ vẫn thêm -s với 'it/he'. Hãy chú ý cả hai.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Sentences often combine a frequency adverb and a third-person -s verb: 'my father often says', 'it ticks softly'. The adverb goes before the main verb, and the verb still takes -s with 'it/he'. Watch both at once.

sampleTranslationVi: Trong bếp nhà chúng tôi có một chiếc đồng hồ cũ trên tường. Nó tròn, màu trắng và kêu tích tắc nhẹ nhàng. Nhưng chiếc đồng hồ này luôn chậm một chút. Mỗi ngày nó chậm mất khoảng năm phút. Nên trong một tuần nó rất sai. Bà tôi mua chiếc đồng hồ này nhiều năm trước. Chúng tôi biết nó chậm, nên chúng tôi cộng thêm mười phút trong đầu. Bố tôi thường nói chiếc đồng hồ nhà bếp nói dối, nhưng ông cười khi nói vậy. Tháng trước tôi đã thay pin, nhưng đồng hồ vẫn chậm. Vấn đề nằm bên trong, không phải pin. Chúng tôi không bao giờ vứt nó đi vì chúng tôi yêu tiếng kêu nhẹ nhàng của nó. Một chiếc đồng hồ mới thì rẻ, nhưng cái này có một câu chuyện. Tôi nghĩ một chiếc đồng hồ chậm lại tốt theo một cách kỳ lạ. Nó dạy tôi rằng thời gian không phải là một cỗ máy hoàn hảo. Đôi khi tôi đang vội, và chiếc đồng hồ chậm khiến tôi thấy bình tĩnh. Nó nói, bạn có nhiều thời gian hơn bạn nghĩ. Tôi luôn mỉm cười khi nhìn nó.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct present simple form: 'It ___ about five minutes every day.'
options: loses
lose
losing
lost
mcQuestions.hint: The subject is 'it' — what ending does the verb need?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses an adverb of frequency correctly?
options: My father says often the clock lies.
My father often says the clock lies.
Often my father say the clock lies.
My father often saying the clock lies.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'often' goes before the main verb 'says', which takes -s for 'my father'.
mcQuestions.hint: Where does 'often' go, and what ending does 'father' need?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses the past simple for last month?
options: Last month I change the battery.
Last month I changed the batterys.
Last month I changed the battery.
Last month I am changed the battery.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'changed' is the correct past simple of 'change'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'but' correctly to show a contrast?
options: A new clock is cheap, but a story this one has has.
A new clock cheap is, but this one story has.
But a new clock is cheap this one has a story.
A new clock is cheap, but this one has a story.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'but' joins two full clauses; the second has subject + verb + object.
mcQuestions.hint: Both clauses after 'but' need a subject and a verb.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence best explains why you do not throw the clock away?
options: We never throw it away because we love its soft sound.
We never throw it away because love soft sound we.
We never throw it away so its soft sound we love.
Because soft sound we never it away throw love.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'never' before the verb and 'because' + full clause are both correct.
mcQuestions.hint: After 'because' you need subject + verb; 'never' goes before the verb.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct present simple: 'It ___ softly on the wall.'
options: tick
ticks
ticking
ticked
mcQuestions.hint: The subject is 'it' — what ending does the verb need?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses the past simple about your grandmother?
options: My grandmother buyed this clock many years ago.
My grandmother buy this clock many years ago.
My grandmother bought this clock many years ago.
My grandmother is bought this clock many years ago.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'bought' is the irregular past simple of 'buy'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence is the most natural reflection on time?
options: It teach me that time is not a perfect machine.
It teaches me that time are not a perfect machine.
It teaches me that time is not a perfectly machine.
It teaches me that time is not a perfect machine.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'It teaches' (-s) and 'time is' (singular) are both correct.
mcQuestions.hint: Check the verb after 'it' and after 'time'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence sounds the most natural about being busy?
options: Sometimes I am in a hurry, and the slow clock makes me feel calm.
Sometimes I am in a hurry, and the slow clock make me feel calm.
Sometimes I am in a hurry, and the slow clock makes me to feel calm.
Sometimes I am in hurry, and the slow clock makes me feel calmly.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'clock makes me feel' (causative: -s + object + bare infinitive) and 'in a hurry' are correct.
mcQuestions.hint: After 'makes me', the next verb has no 'to'; check 'in a hurry'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses an adverb of frequency correctly at the end?
options: I smile always when I look at it.
I always smile when I look at it.
Always I smile when I look at it it.
I always smiling when I look at it.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'always' goes before the main verb: 'I always smile'.
mcQuestions.hint: Where does 'always' go with a main verb?

criticalThinkingQuestion: If your clock was always a few minutes slow, would you fix it, or would you keep it and just remember the difference? Why?
